definition: worthy of respect because of advanced age, dignity, etc.
sentence: At family reunions our venerable grandmother, now past eighty, is accorded great respect.

definition: foolish for a grown person to say or do; childish
sentence: Some seniors think it\'s fun to throw objects at passing cars, but I consider it puerile.

definition: lack of proper seriousness; improper gaiety
sentence: During the assembly George kept giggling; levity for which his teacher later scolded him.

definition: thrifty; not wasteful
sentence: Her frugal attitude allowed her to save a good deal of money at the supermarket.

definition: feeling of intense dislike
sentence: Because of his bigoted remarks, the speaker provoked the antipathy of the audience.

definition: a hobby; supplementary occupation
sentence: Bird watching was an avocation that gave him many hours of pleasure.

definition: one who poses as an expert in a skill or profession for which he is not qualified
sentence: The Great Imposter is a movie about a charlatan who posed successfully as a surgeon.

definition: brave; strong and able
sentence: The doughty naval commander fought against very heavy odds.

definition: to make clear; explain
sentence: You may elucidate what happened after you relax for a while.

definition: lazy
sentence: He was indolent by nature but still blamed the heat wave for his inability to do work.

definition: causing laughter because absurd or ridiculous; laughably absurd
sentence: His answer to the question was so ludicrous that everyone burst outlaughing.

definition: young and inexperienced; immature
sentence: He was surprised when his company hired a callow youth just out of college for the important new position.

definition: merry; cheerful; happy
sentence: Our neighbor is a blithe fellow who tells amusing stories.

definition: favoritism shown to relatives, especially in securing jobs
sentence: Whenever a President appoints a relative to a government position, the cry of nepotism is raised by the opposing party.

definition: to speak evil of; to slander
sentence: I cannot bear to hear you malign sucha good man.

definition: occurring after death; published after the author\'s death
sentence: Only two of Emily Dickinson’s poems were published before she died; the rest were posthumous.

definition: outrageously evil or wicked
sentence: The convict was severely punished for his heinous crime.

definition: kept secret or hidden especially for some illegal purpose; secretive
sentence: The clandestine activities of the terrorist group were never discovered by the police.

definition: to disprove or demonstrate the falsity of something
sentence: The district attorney summoned many witnesses to refute the testimony of the defendant.

definition: hasty, hurried or not thorough
sentence: His cursory studying of the material could not give him a full understanding of the subject.

definition: quiet, reserved, or uncommunicative
sentence: Because she was so reticent, very few people got to know her well.

definition: hard work; exhausting labor; agony
sentence: His family found that the travail of serving ten years in a prison camp had changed him greatly.

definition: wealth, luxury, or abundance.
sentence: She was amazed at the opulence of her neighbors.

definition: difficult, strenuous, or hard to achieve
sentence: Climbing the tree proved to be an arduous task but not a particularly dangerous one.

definition: practically worthless; petty; trifling
sentence: I complained not because of the paltry three cents that I was overcharged but because of the principle of the thing.

definition: to carry off by force
sentence: The kidnappers abducted the child from her home.

definition: easily managed, taught, or controlled
sentence: The horse was tractable as long as he was going in the direction of the barn; otherwise he was unmanageable.

definition: to do away with or destroy; to blot out completely
sentence: He tried to obliterate from his memory any trace of that horrible day.

definition: extraordinary in size or amount; enormous
sentence: Anyone who has orbited the Earth has performed a prodigious task.

definition: having or showing great pride in oneself and disdain, contempt, or scorn for others
sentence: He seemed at first to be rather cold and haughty, but we came to realize that this was due mainly to his shyness.

definition: Evenness of mind or temper; calmness; composure
sentence: When one becomes engaged in an argument it is best to retain one’s equanimity in order to keep the dispute from exploding into something bigger.

definition: To irritate or make bitter; to make more violent
sentence: The line of questions so exacerbated the witness that he refused to answer.

definition: A tip; payment for service for which no bill is offered
sentence: A gratuity of 10 to 15 percent of the check is customary in many restaurants.

definition: Skeptical; doubtful; too extraordinary to be believed
sentence: The teacher was incredulous when he heard the amazing excuse.

definition: Talkative; chatty
sentence: The lawyer’s loquacious summation annoyed his client, who could see that the jury was losing attention.

definition: Deserving blame; wrong
sentence: The man did not find the action of his enemy as reprehensible as he thought he would.

definition: Strict; rigid; exacting
sentence: The Dean imposed a curfew of 7:00, the most stringent one in the history of the school.

definition: Habitually silent; not given to conversation
sentence: John Wayne often portrays taciturn characters in his movies.

definition: Present everywhere
sentence: The ubiquitous pizza parlors seem to be feeding America.

definition: Having lost its spirit or zest; spiritless
sentence: The guest speaker made one of the most vapid speeches the audience had ever heard.

definition: Overly or unduly careful about small details; extremely careful
sentence: She was extremely meticulous about her clothes and spent hours dressing for a party.

definition: Unselfish concern for the welfare of others; selflessness
sentence: Altruism is a virtue that very few people possess.

definition: To improve or make better
sentence: Despite the Mayor\'s best efforts, he could not ameliorate the situation in the ghetto.

definition: Characteristic of the country and country people; simple and unsophisticated
sentence: His rustic manners seemed out of place in the elegant setting of the hotel dining room.

definition: To make fun of; to scorn
sentence: The teacher told the obnoxious student not to deride his fellow classmates.

definition: Lacking self confidence; timid; shy
sentence: We discovered that beneath his diffident Personality there was an active and inquiring mind.

definition: To exclude someone from a social group by general consent
sentence: Although he was found innocent of treason, Aaron Burr was ostracized by society and left this country to live in England.

definition: Savage; cruel; fierce; warlike
sentence: The debate, which might have been very interesting, was marred by a truculent exchange of personal abuse.

definition: To clear of suspicion, as of a charge of wrongdoing
sentence: Since he has been fully vindicated by the investigation, he will resume his old position in the government.

definition: Eager and ready to fight; quarrelsome
sentence: He became much less pugnacious after he picked fights with boys who gave him sound beatings.

definition: looking dangerous, threatening, disagreeable, repellent
sentence: The glow from the distant lighthouse cast a forbidding hue over the ocean.

definition: foolish, silly, lacking sense or meaning
sentence: We were trying not to sound inane as we spoke to the art historian, but we really knew nothing about the subject.

definition: to become accustomed to something, difficult, painful, etc.
sentence: I never became inured to running in the early morning.

definition: the complete absence of government, political disorder or violence, disorder in any activity
sentence: “If he gets up, we’ll all get up. It’ll be anarchy,” is a famous line from The Breakfast Club.

definition: hostility, antagonism, hatred; the bitter feelings of an enemy
sentence: Many Americans cannot help but feel enmity toward Osama Bin Laden and his terrorist network.

definition: any office or position that brings profit or advantage without involving much work or responsibility
sentence: No one would ever need to retire from a job that is a sinecure.

definition: not expressed or declared openly, but implied or understood
sentence: Her nod was enough tacit approval for me to understand her message.

definition: to trick into doing something, to lead on with deception
sentence: The salesman had a reputation for inveigling his customers into buying things they did not intend to buy.

definition: anything that is or seems to be out of its proper time in history
sentence: For a girl to call her boyfriend her “beau” is anachronistic in 2011.

definition: too great a desire to have wealth; greed for riches
sentence: His avarice increased with age, until it became an uncontrollable obsession for wealth.

definition: to excuse, forgive, or pardon; to overlook an offense
sentence: His behavior was so outrageous that I cannot see how anyone could even try to condone it.

definition: made trite and commonplace by overuse; stale
sentence: His writing style is weakened by repeated use of hackneyed phrases.

definition: required; absolutely necessary; compulsory
sentence: It is imperative that you submit your application before May 1.

definition: worldly, (as distinguished from heavenly); commonplace
sentence: Now that we have discussed all those profound philosophical problems, let\'s get down to the mundane job of preparing dinner.

definition: calm; peaceful; undisturbed
sentence: Although the scene was still placid, I knew that before long we were in for some wild excitement.

definition: not genuine or true; lacking in authenticity; fake
sentence: The document on which we had intended to base our case turned out to be spurious--a crude counterfeit.

definition: marked by secrecy or concealment; furtive; sly
sentence: Creeping stealthily, through the underbrush, the soldiers approached their enemies.

definition: refined; polite and courteous in a smooth, polished way; suave
sentence: I admired the urbane ease with which he handled the difficult situation.

definition: a social outcast; one despised by society
sentence: In Lord of the Flies, Piggy quickly became the pariah of the small society that formed on the island.

definition: to violate the sacredness of something; to profane; to treat as if not sacred
sentence: Vandals had desecrated the church by carving their initials on the large wooden doors.

definition: loud, noisy, or vehement in making one\'s feelings known
sentence: The judge ordered the vociferous spectator to be thrown out of the courtroom

definition: a mild or pleasant expression substituted for one that is harsh or blunt
sentence: Instead of calling Meredith "fat," he tactfully employed the euphemism "pleasingly plump."

definition: a scarcity or lack; too small a supply or shortage
sentence: When the crops failed, there was a dearth of grain for flour, and bread prices skyrocketed.

definition: a person or thing that comes before to announce or give an indication of what is to follow
sentence: The first robin is a well-known harbinger of spring. The first vulture circling is a harbinger of something else.

definition: angry, wrathful, incensed
sentence: He is usually even-tempered, but he becomes irate when he encounters rudeness.

definition: the highest point, peak; culmination
sentence: At the very pinnacle of success, he enjoyed wealth, admiration from others, and widespread affection.

definition: stubbornly disobedient or rebellious; hard to handle; unruly
sentence: We cannot allow one recalcitrant student to disrupt the work of the entire class.

definition: dirty from neglect; wretched
sentence: Jakob Riis photographed the awful living conditions in the squalid tenements of New York.

definition: unclear; vague; cloudy, misty
sentence: His ideas were so nebulous that no one could agree or disagree with him.

definition: to speak out against strongly and openly; to denounce
sentence: The graduation speaker decried the tendency of too many Americans to seek wealth before all else.

definition: to praise highly; to laud
sentence: The speaker extolled the doctor for his great contributions to mankind.

definition: that which seems to be with someone from birth; existing naturally rather than acquired
sentence: His good taste and courtesy are not the result of training but rather are innate.

definition: a perplexing or seemingly unexplainable matter or person
sentence: The many aspects of his changing personality make Tom an enigma to me.

definition: a continuing and bitter hate or ill will; deep spite and malice.
sentence: Unfortunately, the debate between them was marked by personal rancor rather than honest discussion of the issues.

definition: a violent verbal attack; strong criticism, insult curses, etc.
sentence: It is possible to wage a hard-hitting political campaign without resorting to the use of invectives.

definition: keenness and quickness in understanding and dealing with a situation; shrewdness
sentence: In the 19th century, many fortunes were made by men with great business acumen.

definition: not easily moved to pity or sympathy; hard- hearted, stubborn or obstinate
sentence: In spite of all our efforts to persuade him, he remained obdurate.

definition: practical; concerned with actual, everyday things rather than with theory or speculation
sentence: Rather than listening to the politicians explain the theories of poverty, he had the pragmatic problem of getting food on the table.

definition: fearlessly daring; bold; arrogantly insolent
sentence: In this time of crisis, we need imaginative, bold and even audacious leadership.

definition: common, unoriginal, drearily predictable
sentence: The lecturer’s banal comments bored the audience to tears.

definition: Responsible for doing something wrong; deserving blame; guilty
sentence: Anyone who took any part in the conspiracy was considered equally culpable.

definition: Deserving disgrace or shame; dishonorable; despicable
sentence: The traitor spent the last years of his life in ignominious exile.

definition: showing off boastfully; boastful
sentence: The man displayed his expensive new suit in a very ostentatious manner.

definition: changes in fortune; ups and downs in circumstances
sentence: You must learn to face the many vicissitudes of life.

definition: cheerful willingness or eagerness
sentence: Since Bob needed a job to pay for his college expenses, he accepted with alacrity the offer.

definition: in a state of decline or decay; deteriorating
sentence: When people lose interest in literature, it\'s a sign that the society is becoming decadent.

definition: Talkative
sentence: The garrulous cab driver kept up a steady stream of chatter.

definition: Reckless, boldness; foolish disregard for danger
sentence: In 1941, Japanese militarists had the temerity to risk the future of their country on the wild gamble of a knockout blow on Pearl Harbor.

definition: composed of materials gained from many sources, systems, etc.
sentence: The statesman did no believe in only one political philosophy, but rather took a more eclectic view of world affairs.

definition: a thing or person accursed or damned; a thing or person greatly detested; a formal curse
sentence: After almost 200 years, the name of Benedict Arnold is still anathema to Americans.

definition: noble in mind; high-souled; generous in overlooking injury or insult; rising above pettiness or meanness
sentence: Churchill emphasized that we should be firm in war, defiant in defeat, and magnanimous in victory.

definition: a person who hates or mistrusts all people
sentence: Because of his hateful stares and habitual silence, the man had the reputation of being a misanthrope.

definition: unreasonably cheap or stingy
sentence: He had become so accustomed to a parsimonious way of living that he denied himself even the simplest comforts.

definition: a person who has or professes to have great learning; actual or self-professed authority
sentence: During the week of the Super Bowl, many so-called pundits are always telling you in advance which team will win the game.

definition: offering unnecessary or unwanted advice or services; meddlesome, especially in an overbearing way
sentence: To disguise the fact that he was performing no useful function, he developed an officious manner of intruding into everyone else’s work

definition: bold; fearless; dauntless; very brave
sentence: All honors should go to the intrepid and highly trained men who are exploring outer space.

definition: very sad or mournful, especially in a way that seems exaggerated or ridiculous
sentence: His lugubrious expression was intended to suggest that he, more than anyone else, understood the sadness of the occasion.

definition: intended for or understood by only a chosen few beyond the knowledge and understanding of most people
sentence: The lecture was too esoteric to be appreciated by most people in the audience.

definition: arrogant; haughty; seeking to dominate; overbearing
sentence: He is so inflated with the sense of his own importance that he adopts an imperious manner toward his subordinates.

definition: distressing to the feelings; touching; appealing to the emotions; affecting
sentence: Who can remain unmoved by the poignant scene in which Sidney Carton sacrifices his life for the woman he loves.

definition: distasteful; offensive; repulsive
sentence: I find his horrible manners utterly repugnant.

definition: implied but not expressed
sentence: Since mother raised no objection to my idea, I assumed that I had her tacit consent to go ahead with the plan.

definition: involving sympathetic participation by a person in the experiences of another; experienced or endured or enjoyed through imaginative participation in the experiences of another
sentence: Having been too frail to participate in competitive sports, he took vicarious pleasure in his younger brother\'s football heroics.

definition: to evade or conceal the truth; to deviate from the truth; to lie
sentence: Although you may be tempted to prevaricate, in the long run it will be better off if you own up to what you did.

definition: contemptuously proud; haughty; disdainful
sentence: In spite of his supercilious attitude, I refuse to concede that he is our social superior.

definition: to weaken; to deprive of force
sentence: The long stretch of hot, humid weather enervated all of us.

definition: an excess; an overabundance
sentence: The boy has a plethora of energy and inventiveness, which he sometimes directs into mischief.

definition: an original that serves as a model on which later stages are based or judged; an early and typical example
sentence: Salinger\'s brilliant Catcher in the Rye has served as the prototype for many novels about confused teenagers.

definition: to one side; crookedly
sentence: In my efforts to straighten the picture, I knocked the lampshade askew.

definition: to cancel or repeal by authority; to annul
sentence: Since the treaty had proved unworkable, the two nations decided to abrogate it.

definition: to leave to another in a will; to hand down
sentence: My father bequeathed to his children a little money and the glorious example of a life well spent in the service of his country.

definition: to praise highly
sentence: At the testimonial dinner, he was lauded by community leaders for his service to the school.

definition: grossly stupid or dull; coarse
sentence: His attempts to argue about a subject far beyond his depth seemed only to reveal his crass ignorance and conceit.

definition: smug; self satisfied; pleased with oneself
sentence: He had the complacent attitude of a man who considers himself superior to the "common herd."

definition: great misery or distress; great trouble
sentence: The tribulations which he suffered and overcame have made him a wiser, more compassionate person.

definition: to coax or persuade with insincere or false words; to wheedle
sentence: Using rather far-fetched promises, I finally cajoled her into accompanying us to the fair

definition: mental distress due to disappointment or humiliation
sentence: When he did not receive an invitation, he tried unsuccessfully to conceal hisdeep-seated chagrin.

definition: easily taught; responsive; tractable
sentence: Since I expected him to be unruly, I was pleasantly surprised by his gentle, docile behavior.

definition: to waver in mind or opinion
sentence: When you have considered all the factors carefully and have decided what is right, act without vacillating.

definition: marked by wisdom and good judgment
sentence: His sage advice has helped thousands of people deal with their problems.

definition: to deceive
sentence: Only an inexperienced and naive person could be duped into investing money in such a hairbrained scheme.

definition: highest point; pinnacle
sentence: Election to the Presidency was the acme of his long and brilliant career.

definition: to restrain or discourage from action through fear
sentence: Once he had decided where his duty lay, nothing could deter him.

definition: a sermon; a discourse emphasizing moral principles
sentence: Instead of those high-flown homilies, we need some good practical plans.

definition: one who cultivates refined tastes, especially in eating and drinking
sentence: The store specializes in delicacies that will please even the most demanding epicures.

definition: excessive amount
sentence: I have had my surfeit of excuses and evasions; I want action!

definition: traveling from place to place
sentence: Peter became an itinerant folk singer, traveling to college campuses all over the land.

definition: a hiding place
sentence: The conspirators had set up a cache of firearms in the old farmhouse.

definition: to instigate; to spur on
sentence: Only intolerable living conditions could have incited the peasants to rebellion.

definition: to bring to a state of peace or quiet; to satisfy by making concessions or giving in to
sentence: The tyrant can be appeased only by absolute submission to his will.

definition: impulsive; done hastily or rashly
sentence: Because he is impetuous by nature, John often has cause to regret his rash actions.

definition: treachery; breach of trust or faith
sentence: Because he betrayed our country, Benedict Arnold\'s name has become a symbol of perfidy in American history.

definition: a dirty, wretched dwelling place
sentence: It is shocking that the families of the migratory workers had to live in such hovels.

definition: coming into view or being; arrival
sentence: The advent of winter brings thoughts of sleds, ice-skates, and skis.

definition: needy; poor
sentence: The purpose of this new program is to help those indigent people who can\'t help themselves.

definition: impudence; shameless boldness
sentence: How does he have the effrontery to ask me for help when he ignored my requests during my time of need.

definition: to correct; to set right
sentence: It is good to rectify a mistake; it is better not to make it in the first place.

definition: to accumulate; to gather together
sentence: By saving systematically over the years, he amassed a large fortune.
